requiredMachineState.description: Canonical persisted one-shot init result for zero-Pact Java/Spring provider repos. It must capture whether init was completed, not justified, blocked by insufficient boundary evidence, or redirected because existing Pact evidence already exists; identify the provider candidate and HTTP surface; record the existing Pact evidence check; explain the bootstrap justification and narrow write plan; and persist what was written without pretending verification is already grounded or passing.

outcomeTaxonomy.description: pact-init must classify its one-shot bootstrap outcome using this narrow verdict set.

allowedRepoWrites: minimal Pact provider test dependency patching in pom.xml or build.gradle when clearly needed
forbiddenRepoWrites: claims that provider verification is already proven or passing
writePolicy.description: pact-init may write only narrow provider-side bootstrap artifacts and must leave the repo prepared for the normal verification track, not pretend the full verification lifecycle is already complete.

stopConditions.description: Meaningful Pact provider evidence already exists, so init must redirect to the normal verification track instead of scaffolding a parallel setup.
The repo does not provide enough boundary evidence to choose a provider under contract honestly.
The repo exposes weak or internal-only HTTP seams, so Pact bootstrap is not justified right now.

summaryState.description: Human-readable Pact init summary only. Canonical machine state stays in init-state.json.

allowedSharedData: Spring controller and request mapping evidence
